name: Stability zone classification correctness
aim: RunStabilityAssessment must classify CollapseZone correctly for any (ρ, hazard, reservoir)

threshold.rationale: Zone boundaries: safe < 0.83*threshold < warning < threshold < collapse. Any misclassification sends controller into wrong mode

on_exceed: Controller receives wrong zone → 'collapse' zone triggers max damping; wrong zone = either overreaction (capacity waste) or underreaction (congestion crash)

answered_questions.what_was_tested: RunStabilityAssessment zone classification across 30000 random (ρ, hazard, reservoir) triples
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Zone boundaries are exact: effectiveRho = ρ + hazard*0.2 + reservoir*0.1 checked against threshold*0.83 and threshold. Any misclassification is a bug
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Controller applies wrong damping strategy; collapse zone underreaction causes congestion crash; safe zone overreaction wastes capacity

name: Stability margin formula correctness
aim: StabilityMargin must equal 1 - (ρ + hazard*0.2 + reservoir*0.1) when no upstream pressure

threshold.rationale: Direct algebraic identity: margin = 1 - effectiveRho. 1e-10 covers float64 rounding

on_exceed: StabilityMargin miscalculated → Engine.RunControl uses wrong distance-to-saturation → PID tunes deadband incorrectly → control oscillation or stiction

answered_questions.what_was_tested: StabilityMargin formula across 30000 random (ρ, hazard, reservoir) inputs with 0 upstream pressure
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: margin = 1 - effectiveRho is a single subtraction; 1e-10 is generous for float64. Any larger error means the formula changed or has a bug
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Controller bases all decisions on distance-to-saturation. Wrong margin → wrong deadband → either chattering (too tight) or unresponsive (too loose)

name: CollapseRisk monotonicity with utilisation
aim: Higher effective ρ must always produce equal or higher CollapseRisk

threshold.rationale: Sigmoid is monotonically increasing; any regression means cascade amplification or upstream pressure logic inverted a sign

on_exceed: CollapseRisk decreases with load → stability model claims higher load is safer → controller reduces capacity when it should increase it → congestion crash

answered_questions.what_was_tested: CollapseRisk monotonicity across 30000 random (ρ_low, ρ_high) pairs
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: CollapseRisk = sigmoid((effectiveRho - threshold*0.95) / 0.04). Sigmoid is strictly monotone increasing. Any regression is a formula error, not numeric noise
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Stability model gives inverted advice; autopilot reduces capacity under increasing load; guaranteed congestion crash

name: NetworkField finite-state invariant
aim: All cell densities, TotalMass, and TotalVariation must remain finite (no NaN/Inf) for any configuration

threshold.rationale: NaN/Inf in density field = PDE solver divergence. Any single occurrence corrupts the entire network model

on_exceed: PDE solver produces NaN/Inf → density field becomes garbage → all network-coupled load propagation and routing produce meaningless results

answered_questions.what_was_tested: NetworkField finite-state across 10000 random configs (nCells, initRho, noiseAmp, serviceRate), 30 steps each
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: The solver uses Godunov flux, MUSCL limiters, and explicit [0,1] clamping. NaN/Inf can only appear from division by zero in dt() or unguarded floating point operations — any occurrence is a code defect
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Density field contains NaN/Inf → all downstream consumers (topology, routing, network coupling) produce garbage → system-wide control failure

name: NetworkField density bounds [0, 1]
aim: Every cell Rho must remain in [0, 1] after any number of steps

threshold.rationale: fluxStep has explicit clamp: if Rho < 0 → 0; if Rho > 1 → 1. Any OOB means clamp bypassed or applied in wrong order

on_exceed: Density outside [0,1] → Flux(ρ)=ρ(1-ρ) produces negative values → Godunov flux sign inverts → solver diverges catastrophically

answered_questions.what_was_tested: Cell density bounds across 10000 random field configs, 30 steps each
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: fluxStep clamps every cell to [0,1] after update. Any OOB is a code defect, not numeric noise
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Flux function Flux(ρ)=ρ(1-ρ) produces wrong sign when ρ∉[0,1]; GodunovFlux comparison logic breaks; solver produces garbage

name: MPC trajectory cost bounded [0, 1]
aim: MPCHorizonEval.Evaluate must produce trajectory costs within [0, 1] for all inputs

threshold.rationale: Trajectory cost is defined as normalised risk-latency in [0,1] by construction (tanh and sigmoid outputs)

on_exceed: Trajectory cost outside [0,1] → composite objective function produces wrong scale factor → MPC steers system away from setpoint

answered_questions.what_was_tested: MPCHorizonEval.Evaluate across 20000 random (ρ, trend, μ, pid, scale) tuples
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: The cost function uses tanh(Wq*2) ∈ [0,1] and sigmoid ∈ [0,1], weighted sum with w_lat+w_risk=1.0, so output is mathematically in [0,1]
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: MPC cost normalisation is broken; optimizer cannot compare trajectories correctly; control quality degrades unpredictably

name: PID saturation limit safety
aim: PIDController.Update must never exceed [OutputMin, OutputMax] for any gains/setpoint/measurement

threshold.rationale: OutputMin/OutputMax are hard actuator limits — any violation is unacceptable

on_exceed: PID output exceeds configured bounds → scale factor exceeds physical actuator range → control system instability or hardware damage

answered_questions.what_was_tested: PIDController.Update across 500000 random (kp,ki,kd,sp,pv) tuples, 10 steps each
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: PID has explicit clamp: output = max(OutputMin, min(output, OutputMax)). Any violation means clamp is bypassed or order-of-operations wrong
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Actuator commanded beyond physical range; in load-balancing context, scale factor goes beyond [0.5, 3.0] bounds → other safety checks cascade-fail

name: MPC adjusted scale factor bounds [0.5, 3.0]

threshold.rationale: Hard clamp at line: adjusted = math.Max(0.5, math.Min(adjusted, 3.0)). Any OOB means clamp bypassed

on_exceed: MPC scale factor exceeds [0.5, 3.0] → Engine.RunControl amplifies it further → actuator receives unbounded capacity change → system crash

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: MPC has explicit clamp: math.Max(0.5, math.Min(adjusted, 3.0)). Any value outside this range means dead code path or clamp bypass
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Scale factor passes unchecked to Engine.RunControl which applies further amplification — cascading out-of-bounds through the entire control pipeline

name: Trajectory planner feasibility selection
aim: PlanTrajectory must select a feasible candidate when any feasible candidate exists

threshold.rationale: A planner that selects infeasible trajectories when feasible ones exist has broken selection logic

on_exceed: Planner selects infeasible trajectory → system deliberately steered past collapse threshold → congestion collapse

answered_questions.what_was_tested: PlanTrajectory feasibility across 20000 random (ρ, trend, μ) inputs
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: The planner's contract is 'prefer feasible candidates'. Selecting infeasible when feasible exists is a logic error in candidate ranking
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Planner recommends a trajectory that crosses the collapse threshold, causing the Engine to drive the system into overload

name: Bounded state evolution (no divergence)
aim: FluidPlant.Step must never produce NaN/Inf/unbounded Q for any arrival rate over 100 ticks

threshold.rationale: StabilityGain=0.42 ensures dissipative contraction: bounded input → bounded Q. 100 is generous; math says ~12 is steady-state max at arrival=5

on_exceed: Unbounded queue length → MPC operates on divergent state → actuator command has no physical bound → system crash or hardware damage

answered_questions.what_was_tested: FluidPlant.Step across 50000 random arrival rates [0, 5], each run for 100 ticks
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: The plant has dissipative drift -0.42*Q; for bounded arrivals (max 5), steady-state Q ≈ (A-S)/0.42 ≈ 12. Threshold 100 gives 8× headroom for stochastic excursions
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Queue length diverges without bound, MPC and PID operate on meaningless state, all downstream control becomes unstable
answered_questions.is_the_test_deterministic: Yes — gopter seed=42, FluidPlant seed=42, fully reproducible

name: Deterministic reproducibility
aim: Two FluidPlant runs with identical seed+inputs must produce bit-identical state

threshold.rationale: Same seed + same input = same output by construction of math/rand.NewSource

on_exceed: Non-determinism breaks simulation replay, A/B testing, and offline debugging — root cause analysis becomes impossible

answered_questions.what_was_tested: FluidPlant determinism across 20000 seed/arrival combinations, 50 ticks each
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Threshold is exactly 0: Go's math/rand with same source produces identical sequence. Any nonzero diff means external state leaked into the computation
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Simulation replay produces different results → debugging and regression testing become unreliable
answered_questions.is_the_test_deterministic: Yes — gopter seed=99, plant seeds generated deterministically

name: Hazard non-negativity invariant
aim: FluidPlant.Z (hazard) must stay >= 0 for all inputs at every tick

threshold.rationale: Hazard is a damage accumulator — negative hazard is physically meaningless (you cannot un-damage hardware)

on_exceed: Negative hazard → BaseService/(1+α*q+β*Z) increases service rate → physics model rewards damage → MPC exploits this to steer into collapse

answered_questions.what_was_tested: FluidPlant.Z non-negativity across 50000 random arrival rates, 100 ticks each
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Z represents cumulative structural degradation. updateHazard clamps Z>=0 explicitly. If the clamp is removed or bypassed, Z goes negative under healing+drag
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Service rate formula S = BaseService/(1+α*Q+β*Z) produces S > BaseService when Z < 0 → degraded system appears faster than healthy one
